for.p: the UK public authorities as well as the courts

to.p: treat the Articles in Schedule 1 of the Act, as having the force of law in the UK

for.p: Courts, Administrative bodies, and interpreters of legislation

to.p: construe the specific terms in the Vienna Convention,('agents of the receiving State' includes constables; 'national' means UK and colonies; etc.) in accordance with definitions that are provided in this subsection

condition.p: interpreting and applying the Vienna Convention in UK legal proceedings

to.p: treat any waiver by the head of a mission (or acting head) as a waiver by the state.

for.p: Her Majesty by Order in Council

to.p: withdraw privileges and immunities from the mission or another state or its members

condition.p: the immunities or the privileges accorded to a UK mission or its members in the State are less than those conferred by the Act.

for.p: Courts and parties in proceedings under the Act

to.p: treat a certificate issued by or under the authority of Secretary of State as conclusive evidence of the stated fact.

condition.p: [WHEN] a question arises in proceedings about entitlement to any privilege or immunity under this act

for.p: the officials or the government making recommendations to Her Majesty

to.p: ensure a draft Order under section 2 is laid before and approved by resolution by each House of Parliament

condition.p: making the recommendation to Her Majesty in Council

to.p: annul a statutory instrument containing an Order under Section 3

condition.p: [WHEN] a resolution for annulment is passed.

for.p: Her Majesty by Order in Council

to.p: vary or revoke any Order made under the Act
extend diplomatic immunities and exemptions to persons or goods as provided in any such agreement

condition.p: [WHEN] such an agreement was in force at the commencement of this Act and remains in force

to.p: publish and amend notices in the London, Edinburgh and Belfast Gazettes specifying States and classes of persons subject to applicable agreements

condition.p: [WHEN] such agreements are either in force or amended

for.p: Her Majesty by Order in Council

to.p: appoint the day this Act comes into force
